title: NDBC Salinity Map
text:
Code
library(dplyr)
library(httr2)
library(htmltools)
library(knitr)
library(leaflet)
library(readr)
library(scales)
library(viridisLite)

# Station list and parameters mirror ndbc_fetch_airflow_dag.py
inwater_params <- c("sea_water_practical_salinity", "sea_water_temperature")

ndbc_stations <- list(
  list(name = "bkyf1", long_name = "Buoy_Key"),
  list(name = "bnkf1", long_name = "Butternut_Kay"),
  list(name = "bobf1", long_name = "Bob_Allen_Key"),
  list(name = "bwsf1", long_name = "Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "dkkf1", long_name = "Duck_Key"),
  list(name = "gbtf1", long_name = "Garfield_Bight"),
  list(name = "hcef1", long_name = "Highway_Creek"),
  list(name = "jbyf1", long_name = "Joe_Bay"),
  list(name = "jkyf1", long_name = "Johnson_Key"),
  list(name = "lbsf1", long_name = "Little_Blackwater_Sound"),
  list(name = "lmdf1", long_name = "Little_Madeira_Bay"),
  list(name = "lrkf1", long_name = "Little_Rabbit_Key"),
  list(name = "lsnf1", long_name = "Long_Sound"),
  list(name = "mdkf1", long_name = "Middle_Key"),
  list(name = "mukf1", long_name = "Murray_Key"),
  list(name = "pkyf1", long_name = "Peterson_Key"),
  list(name = "tbyf1", long_name = "Terrapin_Bay"),
  list(name = "thrf1", long_name = "Thursday_Point"),
  list(name = "wrbf1", long_name = "Whipray_Basin")
)

# 25-hour lookback window
end_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")
start_time <- format(Sys.time() - 25 * 3600, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC")

fetch_latest_salinity <- function(station_info) {
  param <- "sea_water_practical_salinity"
  url <- sprintf(
    "https://erddap.secoora.org/erddap/tabledap/gov-nps-ever-%s.csv?time%%2Clatitude%%2Clongitude%%2C%s&time%%3E=%s&time%%3C=%s",
    station_info$name,
    param,
    start_time,
    end_time
  )

  resp <- request(url) |>
    req_perform()

  df <- read_csv(resp_body_string(resp), show_col_types = FALSE)
  names(df) <- c("time", "latitude", "longitude", "salinity")
  df <- df[-1, ] |>
    mutate(
      time = as.POSIXct(time, format = "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", tz = "UTC"),
      latitude = as.numeric(latitude),
      longitude = as.numeric(longitude),
      salinity = as.numeric(salinity)
    ) |>
    filter(!is.na(salinity))

  if (nrow(df) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  latest <- df[which.max(df$time), ]
  tibble(
    station_id = station_info$name,
    station = station_info$long_name,
    location = sprintf(
      "%s (%s)",
      station_info$long_name,
      sub("f1$", "", station_info$name)
    ),
    time = latest$time,
    latitude = latest$latitude,
    longitude = latest$longitude,
    salinity = latest$salinity
  )
}

salinity_data <- bind_rows(lapply(ndbc_stations, fetch_latest_salinity))
report_time <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M %Z")
Data are pulled from SECOORA ERDDAP: using a 25-hour window ending at render time, with the most recent non-missing salinity value kept for each station.
Report generated: 2026-07-07 15:30 EDT
Query window: 2026-07-06T18:30:34Z to 2026-07-07T19:30:34Z
Code
if (nrow(salinity_data) == 0) {
  stop("No salinity data returned from ERDDAP for the current time window.")
}

salinity_data |>
  arrange(desc(time)) |>
  mutate(
    time = format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC"),
    salinity = number(salinity, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) |>
  select(Station = station, `Station ID` = station_id, Time = time, `Salinity (PSU)` = salinity) |>
  kable()

Code
salinity_palette <- colorNumeric(
  palette = c("#2166ac", "#67a9cf", "#d1e5f0", "#fddbc7", "#ef8a62", "#b2182b"),
  domain = salinity_data$salinity,
  na.color = "#999999"
)

leaflet(salinity_data) |>
  addProviderTiles("CartoDB.Positron") |>
  setView(
    lng = mean(salinity_data$longitude),
    lat = mean(salinity_data$latitude),
    zoom = 9
  ) |>
  addCircleMarkers(
    lng = ~longitude,
    lat = ~latitude,
    radius = 8,
    stroke = TRUE,
    weight = 1,
    color = "#333333",
    fillColor = ~salinity_palette(salinity),
    fillOpacity = 0.85,
    label = ~sprintf(
      "%s: %.1f PSU\n%s",
      location,
      salinity,
      format(time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC")
    )
  ) |>
  addLegend(
    position = "bottomright",
    pal = salinity_palette,
    values = salinity_data$salinity,
    title = "Salinity (PSU)",
    opacity = 0.9
  )
Salinity is shown in practical salinity units (PSU, parts per thousand). Lower values (blue) indicate fresher water; higher values (red) indicate more marine conditions.

title: NDBC Salinity Map
text:
Interpolated salinity field
The field below uses DIVAnd (Data-Interpolating Variational Analysis) with bathymetry masking, following the same approach as sfer-mbon-oxygen: ~0.01° horizontal grid resolution (~1 km), correlation length (0.05, 0.05) degrees, and epsilon2 = 1.0. Land is masked using Natural Earth 10 m coastlines plus NOAA BlueTopo 4 m bathymetry (8 m tiles fill gaps); cells with missing bathymetry or elevation ≥ −1 m are treated as land.


Code
obs_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "salinity_obs.csv")
field_file <- file.path("data", "interpolated", "salinity_field.csv")
dir.create(dirname(obs_file), recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

salinity_data |>
  select(longitude, latitude, salinity) |>
  write_csv(obs_file)

status <- system2(
  "scripts/run_interpolate_salinity.sh",
  c(obs_file, field_file),
  stdout = FALSE,
  stderr = FALSE
)
if (!identical(status, 0L)) {
  stop("Julia interpolation failed.")
}

salinity_field <- read_csv(field_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
source("R/plot_salinity_field.R")




Code
if (nrow(salinity_field) == 0) {
  stop("Interpolated salinity field is empty.")
}

plot_salinity_field(salinity_field, salinity_data)






The colored grid shows the DIVAnd-interpolated field; circle markers show the buoy observations used as input.
